description: Benchmark profile definitions used to label competitive runs and prevent tuned-vs-default comparisons.

shared_normalization.shell: posix sh unless platform profile overrides
machine_state: record monitor refresh rate and DPI scale
record AC/battery state and power profile
allow thermal cooldown between long measured suites

sampling.order: randomized terminals inside a fixed workload/profile/platform block
sampling.outlier_policy: predeclare before running; report max and p99 even when outliers are excluded from confidence intervals

default.label: default
default.normalization: only grid, workload command, platform metadata, and run count are forced
default.config_policy: no generated config unless the terminal requires one to launch

normalized.label: normalized
normalized.normalization: use every shared_normalization setting that the terminal supports
normalized.term_policy: compatibility TERM unless a native-terminfo comparison is explicitly selected
normalized.config_policy: generated benchmark config with config hash recorded

native_fast.label: native-fast
native_fast.normalization: start from normalized, then apply documented speed settings and label every deviation
native_fast.term_policy: native TERM when it is part of the terminal's fast path
native_fast.config_policy: generated config plus a deviation list

low_latency.label: low-latency
low_latency.normalization: start from normalized, then minimize repaint/input delay knobs where supported
low_latency.term_policy: native or compatibility TERM must be labeled

feature_heavy.label: feature-heavy
feature_heavy.purpose: performance with common expensive features enabled
feature_heavy.normalization: start from normalized with ligatures, shell integration, hyperlinks, tabs/splits, image support, and IME enabled when supported
feature_heavy.term_policy: native TERM preferred for feature enablement

compatibility.label: compatibility
compatibility.purpose: conservative xterm-like behavior for cross-terminal comparability
compatibility.normalization: start from normalized with advanced protocols disabled where possible
compatibility.config_policy: generated config with native protocols disabled where supported

native_terminfo.label: native-terminfo
native_terminfo.normalization: start from normalized but use each terminal's native TERM and terminfo package
native_terminfo.term_policy: xterm-ghostty, xterm-kitty, wezterm, alacritty, foot, or the closest documented native TERM
native_terminfo.config_policy: generated config plus exact TERM value and terminfo source
